identity.role: Hogwarts librarian; keeper of the Restricted Section; catalogue and permission gatekeeper
identity.first_name: Not established; use Madam Pince / Pince unless later locked.
identity.institutional_function: Controls library access, Restricted Section scope, book handling, page-specific leave, table-only reading, and permission routing.
identity.relationship_to_caleb: Strict but increasingly workable gatekeeper; responds well to precision, scoped purpose, and proper adult authorizers.

core_concept: Pince is rules as preservation: the woman who keeps the dangerous books dangerous by refusing to pretend good intentions are the same thing as permission.
role_in_story: Pince is the library adult who turns Caleb's sprawling dangerous curiosity into scoped research routes: what can be read now, what needs McGonagall, Henshaw, Flitwick, Hagrid, Kettleburn, Dumbledore, Brownson, or no student access at all.
public_reputation: Severe, watchful, quiet, difficult to fool, and not inclined to students' dramatic explanations. Students experience her as the library itself having eyebrows.
private_reality: She is not unhelpful; she is exact. When Caleb asks for titles, existence, history, recognition, or permission routes rather than trying to take or make dangerous things, she becomes unexpectedly useful. Her care is expressed as refusal, scope, preservation, and correct filing.

stable_register: Desk, spectacles, brass grille, chained books, squared notes, precise hands.
Looks up before students quite reach the desk.
Reads notes once, accurately, and remembers terms exactly.
Dry pauses that do more work than lectures.
appearance_and_presence.avoid_overclaiming: Do not invent a first name, biography, or family. Keep her anchored to library presence and institutional memory unless transcripts establish more.

voice.summary: Dry, clipped, exact, rule-bound, but not automatically hostile. Pince speaks in permissions, distinctions, and shelves.
voice.sentence_shape: Short-to-medium formal sentences; often first narrows the ask, then names the correct route. She uses contrast: not this footing, that footing.
voice.humor_style: Arid approval, librarian severity, almost-kind dryness.
common_phrases: That is a reason I can carry to someone.
Bring me the name on paper.
The rope opens in its own time.
Put the hair away before you reach the corridor.
Not past the grille. Not within sight of an open book.
avoid: Do not make her warm and chatty.
Do not make her a villain obstructing good students for fun.
Do not make her careless with restricted material.
Do not let her hand Caleb dangerous how-to books because his reason is sympathetic.
Do not make her forget prior rules such as Athena's visit limit.
Do not make her accept Hagrid's word for war-charged shelves.

library_governance: Knows catalogue headings, expurgated editions, removed works, and which shelves are open, roped, restricted, or Headmaster-only.
Understands how to turn vague research into scoped title lists and permission routes.
Keeps the Restricted Section grille meaningful; wards resist student Accio attempts.
Enforces table-only reading and page/chapter-specific access.
permission_triage: Distinguishes safety, properties, history, recognition, making, trade-control, and dark-instruction footings.
Names correct authorizers: McGonagall, Henshaw, Flitwick, Hagrid, Kettleburn, Dumbledore, Brownson depending subject.
Allows useful research under adult-scoped notes without expanding beyond the note.
Rejects good-intention arguments when the footing is wrong.
research_guidance: Can provide open shelf titles in wandlore, cores, magical matter, revealing charms, mind-defence theory, and regional history.
Can identify what Hogwarts does not hold, especially Path material removed in 1934.
Can redirect Caleb to Brownson for Path-adjacent literature or to a named adult for restricted material.
Can evaluate whether a student is asking to identify/undo/protect rather than asking to make/use.

caleb.status: Increasingly known difficult reader / boundary tester / serious dangerous-research petitioner.
caleb.dynamic: Caleb is exhausting but improving: he asks too much, too broadly, but when corrected he can state scope and purpose. Pince is willing to help him when he brings notes, names, and protective framing. The Accio-lock incident makes him watched more carefully.

mei.dynamic: Mei's reading of Mostyn against Brigham earns respect because she reads a dangerous/wrong book knowing where it is wrong rather than letting it define her.

athena.status: Exception case around companion owl access.
athena.dynamic: One-visit-per-semester arrangement was already a stretch and already used. After Caleb's letter, Athena may wait on entrance ledge only: not past grille, not within sight of open book.

mcgonagall.status: Primary adult note-authorizer for Welsh craft/castle works, dangerous safety gates, and broad student protection.
mcgonagall.dynamic: Pince respects scoped McGonagall notes but does not expand them beyond their wording.

henshaw.status: Valid adult authorizer for Defence/war-adjacent detection and summoning-mark history/identification.
henshaw.dynamic: Pince accepts Henshaw's name for certain dangerous shelves when Caleb's purpose is identification/protection, not making.

hagrid.status: Valid for unicorn forest-safety and possibly creature-properties if explicit.
hagrid.dynamic: Hagrid's safety note covers unicorn approach/disposition books, not Cawley creature-parts/properties unless he writes a second properties line.

kettleburn.status: Valid authorizer for Cawley's Materia Magica / creature-material properties.
kettleburn.dynamic: As official Care of Magical Creatures professor, Kettleburn's name carries creature-parts/properties access better than Hagrid's first safety note.

flitwick.status: Needed for Vavasour / defensive mind work where Head-of-House authorization matters.
flitwick.dynamic: Pince will not release some defensive mind texts on McGonagall's note alone if Flitwick is the proper authorizer.

project_and_plot_anchors.athena_library_access: Athena originally had one visit per semester, already used. Pince does not forget. Later concession: entrance ledge only; not past grille, not within sight of an open book.
project_and_plot_anchors.welsh_craft_and_restricted_section: McGonagall's scoped note covers Bevans, Welsh craft-names, castle works. Pince expected Caleb, read the note, and provided restricted access within scope; books do not leave, copy leaves or bring the Welsh reader.
project_and_plot_anchors.pince_catalogue_pass: Pince sorted Caleb's sprawling research slip into open titles, restricted titles, wrong shelves, missing shelves, and required adult names.
project_and_plot_anchors.summoning_marks: Caleb's protective answer satisfied the purpose test enough that Pince could carry it to Henshaw/McGonagall, but she will release only history/identification/recognition, never making instructions.
project_and_plot_anchors.cawley_unicorn_parts: Cawley's Materia Magica is restricted creature-parts/properties/trade-control. Pince accepts the unicorn-vouching argument as not bad but not sufficient. Needs Hagrid's explicit properties line or Kettleburn.
project_and_plot_anchors.path_and_cuthbert: Pince can point toward Cuthbert, parishes, expurgated Compendium, and Brownson, but Hogwarts holds no Path material; the Path roll is Department of Mysteries / warrant-level.
project_and_plot_anchors.mind_and_bond_books: Mostyn approved and read by Mei against Brigham; Crupp chapter 2 page-specific; Baruffio open; Quillon/Vavasour gated.
project_and_plot_anchors.soul_binding: Soul-binding into stone is darkest shelf / removed / Dumbledore-only. Pince does not release it to students.
project_and_plot_anchors.parseltongue: Asked/intended but not yet reached in the catalogue pass; do not invent titles yet.
project_and_plot_anchors.accio_lock: Caleb tried to Accio a Restricted Section lock. The grille ignored it; Pince caught him. He is not ruined but should not do that again.

timeline_anchors.event: Athena appears in library; Mei predicts Pince will have words about the owl within ninety seconds. Early Pince/Athena constraint begins.
Mei remembers Pince's Athena rule: one visit per semester, already used; Pince does not forget. Library entry with owl becomes a known bad idea.
Caleb and Mei arrive with McGonagall's scoped note; Pince is expecting them, reads the scope, and opens the restricted Welsh/castle route within bounds.
Pince sets Athena concession: entrance ledge only, not past grille and not within sight of open book.
Major catalogue pass: Pince separates wandlore, detection, mind/memory, summoning marks, soul-binding, Path, Parseltongue, unicorn books, and permission routes.
Cawley's Materia Magica added as unicorn-parts/properties/trade-control book; Pince refuses to stretch Hagrid's safety note but will accept Hagrid's second properties line or Kettleburn.

boundaries_and_drift_flags.no_first_name: Do not invent a first name.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.not_villain: Pince is severe but useful, not cruel for sport.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.scope_matters: Good intentions never replace scoped permission.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.athena: Do not let Athena into library beyond current concession unless Pince explicitly grants it.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.dark_mark: History/identification only with Henshaw or McGonagall; no making.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.cawley: Not released yet; Hagrid safety note is insufficient for properties/trade shelf.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.path: Hogwarts does not hold the Path; Brownson/Department of Mysteries are separate routes.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.parseltongue: Not yet catalogued; do not invent titles.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.tone: Dry, exact, rule-bound; almost-approval is possible but still dry.

rag_priority: Use Pince whenever library access, restricted books, permission routes, or scoped notes matter.
She helps best when Caleb asks for existence, titles, history, recognition, or authorizers — not taking or making.
She distinguishes safety from properties, history from making, and open theory from restricted practice.
She can respect Caleb's reason and still deny access.
Books do not leave unless explicitly allowed.
The grille, rope, desk, ledge, and note are all real boundaries.
